Vacation yard care in Guelph

Travel without the overgrown-lawn giveaway. We keep everything cut, watered and looking lived-in.

Get a Free Quote Call or Text
While you’re away

The yard that says somebody’s home

An uncut lawn is the clearest signal a house is empty. Instead of asking a neighbour for favours, book us for the exact dates you’re gone — a week, a month, or the whole summer at the cottage.

  • Lawn mowed on schedule, trimming and blowdown included
  • Gardens and planters watered as needed
  • Flexible start and end dates — pay only for the visits you need
  • A text update after each visit if you want one
Local know-how

Why an untouched lawn gives an empty house away

An overgrown lawn is the clearest signal a house is sitting empty — insurance companies and police community programs both list it among the top tells for break-ins. Keeping the lawn cut, edges clean and the property visibly cared for while you travel isn't a luxury; it's the cheapest home-security measure there is.

There's a turf-health side too. Grass that jumps from 3 inches to 6 while you're away can't just be scalped back down — cutting more than a third of the blade at once shocks the lawn brown for weeks. Regular cuts on your normal schedule mean you come home to your lawn, not a recovery project.

We already run weekly routes through every corner of Guelph, so vacation coverage slots straight into the system: same crew, same standard, whether you're gone for a long weekend, a month at the cottage, or a full season abroad. Add watering checks and we'll keep new sod, seed or planters alive too.

Common questions

Frequently asked

How much notice do you need before a trip?

A few days is plenty during the season — we're already routing through your area weekly. For full-summer or multi-month coverage, a week or two of notice lets us lock your schedule in.

Can you water my plants and new sod too?

Yes — watering checks for planters, gardens and new sod or seed can be added to visits, following Guelph's outdoor watering schedule so you don't come home to a bylaw notice either.

Will you send me updates while I'm away?

If you'd like them — a quick text or photo after visits is no problem. Plenty of clients just prefer to land, look at the lawn, and see that everything's handled.

What if it rains or the grass barely grows?

Same policy as our regular clients: rain shifts your cut to the next dry day, and in slow-growth stretches we'll skip and tell you rather than bill for a cut the lawn didn't need.

Do you offer this year-round?

Through the growing season, April to November. Many vacation clients also keep us on for spring or fall cleanup so the property is handled shoulder-to-shoulder with their travel.

Is there a minimum commitment?

No contracts here either — book coverage for a single week away or the whole summer. When you're back, service simply stops or rolls into a regular schedule if you've gotten used to it.
